nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Omer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ahmed, Mohamed, Mohammed, Tarig, Mohammad, Murad, Abdirahman, Abdul, Abdulla, Abdulrahim, Ali, Amin.

variants.name: Omar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Mohamed, Ali, Omar, Ahmed, Ahmad, Hassan, Abdul, Husam, Khalid, Mohammad, Mohd.

Muslim: from the Arabic personal name Omar of uncertain etymology, found almost exclusively among Sunni Muslims. It is thought to be related to Arabic ʿāmir ‘prosperous, full of life, large, substantial’ (see Amir 2). The root word ʿumr means ‘life’. ʿUmar bin al-Khaṭṭāb was the second of the ‘rightly guided’ caliphs (ruled 634–44); he was known by the title al-Fārūq ‘the distinguisher’, i.e. one who distinguishes between truth and falsehood. Compare Omer and Umar .
Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived by a group of elm trees, from the collective form of om ‘elm’.

nameMeanings.name: Euphemia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a Late Greek name derived from eu ‘well, good’ + phēnai ‘to speak’. This was the name of various early saints, notably a virgin martyr said to have been burned at the stake at Chalcedon in 307 . It had a limited popularity in some regions in the 16th and 17th centuries and returned to favour in the Victorian period, especially in the pet form Effie . It is little used at present.

en.lifeSummary: When Euphemia St Omer was born in 1114, in Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Guillaume de Saint-Omer, was 30 and her mother, Melisende Picquigny, was 39. She married Baudouin de Bailleul from 1134 to 1138. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1176, at the age of 62.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Euphemia St Omer родилась в 1114 г. в местности сеинт-омир, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Saint-Omer было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Melisende Picquigny, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baudouin de Bailleul с 1134 по 1138. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1176 г. в возрасте 62 лет.
